definitions.text: Like this, that, these, those; used to make a comparison with something implied by context.
examples: I’ve never seen such clouds in the sky before.
Really? Unfortunately, my mind refuses to bring any such memory forward.
the above address or at such other address as may be provided

definitions.text: Used as an intensifier roughly equivalent to very much (of), quite or rather.
examples: Unfortunately, it wasn't such a good holiday as we thought it would.
I spent such a lot last night (that) now I can't even afford a burger.

definitions.text: A person, a thing, people, or things like the one or ones already mentioned.
examples: Such is life, such as it is.
When applied to women, 'handsome' is used mostly of such as are middle-aged or elderly.

definitions.text: Something being indicated that is similar to something else.
